sections.heading: What is WorkManager?
sections.content: WorkManager is Android Jetpack’s library for deferrable, guaranteed background work. It ensures tasks run even if the app is killed or the device restarts, persisting work in a database. Unlike coroutines or Services, it handles lifecycle and system constraints automatically.

sections.heading: When to Use WorkManager
items: Upload logs, analytics, or user content when network is available
Sync data periodically or on connectivity restore
Process images or media files in background
Chain tasks where step B depends on step A
Tasks with constraints: network, charging, storage

sections.heading: Core Components
sections.content: Worker/CoroutineWorker (doWork logic), WorkRequest (OneTime or Periodic), WorkManager instance (enqueue/manage), WorkInfo (status/output), Constraints (network, charging, idle, storage), Data (input/output).

sections.heading: Enqueue & Chain Work
items: Output data flows automatically between chained workers

sections.heading: Best Practices
items: Keep Data <10KB; work >10 min requires foreground
Observe WorkInfo in UI, not inside Worker
Use Hilt for dependency injection via @HiltWorker
